result:
# Introducing Smart Drafts

We've all been there: a new doc, a blinking cursor, and that familiar pause before the first sentence arrives. Blank pages are stubborn things. They don't just slow you down — they change what you write, nudging you toward safer, smaller ideas just to get *something* on the screen. We built Smart Drafts because the hardest part of writing shouldn't be starting.

Starting a new document now takes one click. Smart Drafts reads your title and the context of your recent work — the projects you're in, the docs you've touched this week, the threads you're part of — and suggests a tailored outline to build from. A product brief gets problem, proposal, risks, and rollout. A retro gets wins, misses, and next steps. A one-pager gets a one-pager's shape. The outline is yours the moment it appears: edit it, reorder it, throw half of it out. It's a running start, not a rulebook.

This is the first step toward something bigger. Next up is **custom voice training**, so your drafts don't just arrive structured — they arrive sounding like you, or like your team. We're rolling that out to early-access workspaces in the coming weeks. In the meantime, open a new doc and give Smart Drafts a try. We think you'll like how the page feels a little less blank.
